learning_outcomes.description: demonstrate knowledge of combinatorial enumeration techniques covered in the course.
demonstrate knowledge of the graph theoretical concepts and results covered in the course.
demonstrate an understanding of the classification of surfaces.
demonstrate knowledge of the concepts and construction techniques in combinatorial design theory covered in the course.
be able to produce sound mathematical proofs of results in discrete mathematics.
be able to solve problems that require the application of discrete mathematical techniques presented in the course.

assessments.task_description:
There will be two invigilated quizzes, each quiz will be 40 minutes long. Quizzes are closed book examinations. No written materials permitted. The use of AI is not allowed.

Quiz 1 will take place on Thursday in Week 4 at 8am. See Blackboard for venue details. Quiz 2 will take place on Thursday in Week 11 at 8am. See Blackboard for venue details.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: Quizzes are completed within the scheduled class time. There are no late submissions as quizzes are completed in class.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.task_description: This is a series of two assignments. Each assignment requires you to answer a set of questions and submit your written solutions by the due date.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submit electronically in Gradescope, accessible through the course Blackboard site. More details on the submission process will be provided on the course Blackboard page.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description: The end of semester examination in this course will be held during the end of semester examination period. It will be an in-person exam held on campus.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

learning_activities.topic:
Lectures

Lectures define the course material. They set out the basic theory and demonstrate techniques for problem solving. They cover all the core material required for the course. They are also used to provide administrative information for the course. During the lectures, students are expected to answer or ask questions when opportunities arise.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Revision week
learning_activities.topic:
Independent Study

The expected workload for a 2 unit course at UQ is 10 hours per week (on average). In addition to time spent at lectures and applied classes, students are expected to revise the course material, work through problem sets, and complete assignments.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06

In addition to time spent at lectures and applied classes, students are expected to revise the course material, work through problem sets, and complete assignments.

learning_activities.topic:
Applied Classes

Applied classes provide students with an opportunity for individual assistance. Students will be given sets of problems to complete during applied classes, giving them the opportunity to practice mathematical techniques with assistance available from a Casual Academic and/or from their peers. The applied classes also provide an opportunity to ask questions about other aspects of the course, including assignments.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06
